# docsfy

> AI-powered documentation generator that creates polished, Mintlify-quality static HTML sites from GitHub repositories

## Getting Started

- [Introduction](introduction.html): Overview of docsfy, what it does, why it exists, and how it uses AI CLI tools to generate professional documentation from GitHub repositories
- [Quickstart](quickstart.html): Get docsfy running in under 5 minutes with Docker Compose, generate your first documentation site, and view the results
- [Installation](installation.html): Detailed installation options including Docker, Docker Compose, and local development setup with uv and Python 3.12+

## Configuration

- [Environment Variables](environment-variables.html): Complete reference for all environment variables including AI_PROVIDER, AI_MODEL, AI_CLI_TIMEOUT, authentication keys, and logging settings
- [AI Providers](ai-providers.html): How to configure and switch between supported AI providers: Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and Cursor Agent, including authentication options for each
- [Docker Deployment](docker-deployment.html): Dockerfile structure, docker-compose.yaml configuration, volume mounts, health checks, and production deployment considerations

## Usage

- [Generating Documentation](generating-docs.html): How to trigger documentation generation for a GitHub repository via the API, including public and private repo support
- [Serving Documentation](serving-docs.html): How to serve generated documentation directly from docsfy or download as a tar.gz archive for self-hosting
- [Incremental Updates](incremental-updates.html): How docsfy tracks repository changes via commit SHA and intelligently regenerates only affected pages for efficiency

## Architecture

- [Generation Pipeline](generation-pipeline.html): Deep dive into the four-stage pipeline: repository cloning, AI planning (plan.json), AI content generation, and HTML rendering
- [AI CLI Integration](ai-cli-integration.html): How docsfy invokes AI CLI tools, the ProviderConfig pattern, subprocess execution, JSON response parsing strategies, and timeout handling
- [Storage & Database](storage-and-database.html): SQLite database schema for project metadata, filesystem layout for cached markdown and rendered HTML sites, and the search index
- [HTML Renderer](html-renderer.html): Jinja2 templating, bundled CSS/JS assets, sidebar navigation, dark/light theme toggle, client-side search, code highlighting, and responsive design

## API Reference

- [POST /api/generate](api-generate.html): Start documentation generation for a GitHub repository URL, including request body format and response structure
- [GET /api/status](api-status.html): List all projects and their current generation status (generating, ready, or error)
- [GET /api/projects/{name}](api-projects.html): Retrieve project details including last generated timestamp, commit SHA, and list of generated pages
- [DELETE /api/projects/{name}](api-projects-delete.html): Remove a project and all its generated documentation from the server
- [GET /api/projects/{name}/download](api-projects-download.html): Download the complete generated documentation site as a .tar.gz archive for self-hosting
- [GET /docs/{project}/{path}](api-docs-serving.html): Serve generated static HTML documentation pages directly from the docsfy server
- [GET /health](api-health.html): Health check endpoint for container orchestration and monitoring

## Development

- [Contributing](contributing.html): Development setup, pre-commit hooks (ruff, mypy, flake8, gitleaks), tox test runner, code quality standards, and how to submit changes
- [Technology Stack](tech-stack.html): Overview of core technologies: FastAPI, Jinja2, SQLite, hatchling, uv, highlight.js, and client-side search implementation
